methodology_long: Rankings are generated by a shadow roto model. Each week, all twelve teams are scored across the fourteen active categories (R, HR, RBI, SB, AVG, OBP, SLG, W, K, ERA, WHIP, K/BB, QS, SV+H) based on an estimate of each team's true strength — not by their actual head-to-head matchup result, which depends on schedule luck. Each team receives 12 points for first in a category down to 1 point for last; the Power Score is the sum (max 168, min 14), and that's the rank. True strength is estimated with a Bayesian prior (the preseason projection) blended with each completed week's real performance using exponential decay, so recent weeks count more than older ones. Rate categories (ERA, AVG, etc.) are computed from weighted components (e.g., total earned runs over total innings pitched), not from averaging the week-level rates.

rankings.blurb: New season high at 141 power. Beat Burns 9-4-3 with an elite pitching week — 73.1 IP, 4 W, 78 K, 2.45 ERA, 1.12 WHIP, 3.71 K/BB, 8 QS, 7 SV+H. The bats were thin (.206/.289/.389) but the pitching carried it. Now leads the league in SB and ranks top-3 in HR, RBI, R, K, SLG, OBP, AVG, QS, ERA, WHIP — eleven cats in the top-3. The 8-2-3 matchup record is the league's best.

Up to #2 — highest ranking of Spoons' season — after the **biggest matchup blowout of the year**: a 13-1-2 demolition of Duran. Week 13 line: 76 hits, .298 AVG, **19 HR**, 50 RBI, plus 73 IP, 6 W, 79 K, 3.08 ERA, 1.05 WHIP, 7 QS. Now leads the league in W, K, QS, ERA, AND WHIP — FIVE #1 rate-cat finishes. The cumulative cat record (113) is now AHEAD of CLAUDE's (112) — Spoons has won more cats overall this season. The matchup record (8-3-2) ties for league-best.

Drops a spot only because Spoons jumped. Week 13 was another strong week: beat Balls 11-4-1 with 55 hits, .248 AVG, 12 HR, 34 RBI, plus 63 IP, 4 W, 58 K, 3.14 ERA, 1.19 WHIP, 6 QS, 6 SV+H. Now leads the league in R, HR, RBI, SV+H, OBP, AND K/BB — six #1 cats. The pitching gap (12 W, 12 QS, 11 K) is still real but trending the right way. Two consecutive top-5-pitching weeks now.

Lost to CLAUDE 4-9-3. Week 13 line was solid in isolation (.274/.351/.461, 10 HR, 37 R, 68.1 IP, 5 W, 72 K, 2.63 ERA, 1.23 WHIP) — but CLAUDE had a bigger pitching week. Cumulative ranks 2nd in OBP AND SV+H, 3rd in K and K/BB, top-4 in W and AVG. The structural offensive weaknesses (last in RBI, 11th in SB, 10th in HR) keep the ceiling around #4.

Lost to ShawSwank 5-10-1. Week 13 had a quiet offensive line (.262, 6 HR, 33 R) for once. Pitching was decent (57.2 IP, 7 W, 57 K, 3.28 ERA, 1.11 WHIP, 4 QS). Cumulative now leads the league in AVG, ranks 2nd in R and 3rd in RBI. The pitching profile remains the structural cap (11th QS, 9th K, 9th W). Same story all year.

Got upset by Camp 4-10-2 — the biggest upset of W13. The line was decent (.275, 11 HR, 26 RBI, 5 W, 76 K, 4.50 ERA) but Camp had one of their best weeks of the year. Caminero still 8-4-1 in matchups (tied for best). The model has been on Caminero's case all season — the underlying ranks (no top-3 cats) explain why.

Up one after beating Polar 10-4-2. Week 13 offense was excellent (.270/.337/.511, 18 HR — second-most of any team this week, 52 R, 41 RBI) but the pitching was atrocious (53.1 IP, 4 W, 60 K, 5.74 ERA, 1.37 WHIP, 3 QS). Cumulative still leads SLG, 2nd in AVG, 3rd in HR. The K/BB rank stays last in the league (12th). One pitching week away from a real top-5 push but the pattern keeps repeating.

rankings.team_name: Camp of the New Orleans Saints
rankings.blurb: Up one after upsetting Caminero 10-4-2. Week 13 was a complete week: 77 hits, .308 AVG, 12 HR, 53 RBI (most by any team this week!), plus 64.2 IP, 6 W, 64 K, 5.43 ERA, 1.55 WHIP, 5 QS, 6 SV+H. The bats made up for the rough pitching rates. Cumulative ranks 3rd in W, 4th in RBI, 5th in SB. The offensive profile is still ranked 10-11 in R, AVG, SLG — but the trajectory has been steadily up the last 3 weeks.

Up one after a strong 10-5-1 win over Y&Y. Week 13: 55 hits, .241/.351/.452, 12 HR, 37 RBI, 5 SB on offense plus 67 IP, 4 W, 63 K, 3.36 ERA, 1.10 WHIP, 7 SV+H. Cumulative ranks 2nd in W, 3rd in SV+H AND SB, top-4 in QS. The 8-5 matchup record is above the model's #9 rank. The offensive cats (12th AVG, 11th HR/RBI, 11th SLG) keep capping the ceiling.

Drops three after getting crushed by Valdez 4-11-1. Week 13 line was rough: .249/.315/.393, 7 HR, 22 R, plus 63.2 IP, 3 W, 62 K, 5.09 ERA, 1.30 WHIP, 4 QS, **0 SV+H** (third straight week with zero saves+holds). Cumulative ranks 2nd in QS, 5th in K/BB, top-7 in K — pitching profile is still real. But the SV+H drought is now structural (last in league at 12) and offensive cats are slipping. The 3-7-3 matchup record is honest about the team's level now.

Up one out of the cellar for the first time since Week 8. Lost to PO' BOYD 4-10-2 but the offense had real life: 14 HR, .265 AVG, 37 R, 37 RBI. Pitching was bad (52.2 IP, 3 W, 42 K, 5.47 ERA, 1.39 WHIP, 5 QS). Cumulative ranks 3rd in SLG and 5th in HR and SV+H. The 2-11 record stays grim but two recent decent offensive weeks have moved the needle a small amount.

Drops to last after the worst matchup result of the year — a 1-13-2 demolition by Spoons. Week 13 line: .196 AVG (worst by any team this week), 5 HR, 22 R, 19 RBI, ZERO SB, plus 75 IP, 5 W, 66 K, 4.92 ERA, 1.37 WHIP, 6 QS. The structural pitching weaknesses (12th WHIP AND K/BB) and the now-cratered offensive ranks (12th OBP AND SLG, 11th AVG) put Duran below Polar for the first time.
